annotations.label: Logical conjunction (and)

anchor.snippet: and (

annotations.note: The `And` connective is defined in Lean core and used throughout Mathlib (e.g. Mathlib.Logic.Basic).

annotations.label: Conjunction of a set of operands

anchor.snippet: The and of a set of operands is true if and only if all of its operands are true

annotations.note: Iterated conjunction over a finite set is captured by `Finset.inf` over `Prop`/`Bool` or via `∀ x ∈ s, p x`, but no single lemma matches verbatim.

annotations.label: Conjunct

anchor.snippet: An operand of a conjunction is a conjunct

annotations.note: This is informal terminology with no Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Notation for conjunction

anchor.snippet: And is usually denoted by an infix operator

annotations.note: The infix `∧` notation for `And` is defined in Lean core and pervasively used in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Big wedge (iterated conjunction)

anchor.snippet: the conjunction of an arbitrary number of elements

annotations.note: Iterated conjunction is expressed via `∀ x ∈ s, p x` or `Finset.inf`/`List.all`, not by a dedicated `⋀`-style operator.

annotations.label: Classical logical conjunction

anchor.snippet: logical conjunction is an operation on two logical values

annotations.note: Classical two-valued conjunction is realized as Lean's `And` (and `Bool.and` at the boolean level).

annotations.label: Conjunctive identity / empty conjunction

anchor.snippet: The conjunctive identity is true

annotations.note: Lemmas `and_true` and `true_and` (core Lean, used throughout Mathlib) express that `True` is the identity of `∧`.

annotations.label: Truth table of conjunction

anchor.snippet: The truth table of

annotations.note: All four truth-table rows are encoded by `and_true`, `true_and`, `and_false`, `false_and` (core), and on `Bool` by `Bool.and_eq_true`.

annotations.label: Conjunction defined by other operators

anchor.snippet: In systems where logical conjunction is not a primitive

annotations.note: `and_iff_not_or_not : a ∧ b ↔ ¬(¬a ∨ ¬b)` in Mathlib.Logic.Basic (line 420) gives the De Morgan definition via ¬ and ∨.

annotations.label: Conjunction introduction

anchor.snippet: conjunction introduction is a classically valid

annotations.note: `And.intro : a → b → a ∧ b` is the conjunction introduction rule, defined in Lean core and used throughout Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bob likes apples and oranges

anchor.snippet: Here is an example of an argument that fits the form conjunction introduction

annotations.note: Informal pedagogical example with no formal counterpart.

annotations.label: Conjunction elimination

anchor.snippet: Conjunction elimination is another classically valid

annotations.note: `And.left` and `And.right` (Lean core) provide the two elimination rules; widely invoked in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Falsifying a conjunction

anchor.snippet: is proven false by establishing either

annotations.note: `not_and_or : ¬(a ∧ b) ↔ ¬a ∨ ¬b` in Mathlib.Logic.Basic (line 415) encodes that falsifying ∧ amounts to falsifying one conjunct.

annotations.label: Falsity from implication between conjuncts

anchor.snippet: a conjunction can actually be proven false just by knowing about the relation of its conjuncts

annotations.note: `not_and_not_right : ¬(a ∧ ¬b) ↔ a → b` (Mathlib.Logic.Basic line 408) gives the closely related equivalence.

annotations.label: Properties of conjunction

anchor.snippet: commutativity

annotations.note: `and_comm`, `and_assoc`, `and_self`, `and_true`, `true_and` (Lean core, used everywhere in Mathlib) cover the standard algebraic properties of ∧.

annotations.label: Conjunction as arithmetic multiplication

anchor.snippet: logical conjunction works exactly like normal arithmetic multiplication

annotations.note: No Mathlib lemma directly identifies `∧` with multiplication on {0,1}; one could express it via `Bool.toNat` but no such lemma exists verbatim.

annotations.label: Bitwise AND of binary words

anchor.snippet: by taking the bitwise AND of each pair of bits at corresponding positions

annotations.note: Bitwise AND on naturals is `Nat.land` (Lean core; with Mathlib lemmas in Mathlib.Data.Nat.Bitwise).

annotations.label: Bit mask extraction

anchor.snippet: This can be used to select part of a bitstring using a bit mask

annotations.note: Application-level usage of bitwise AND for masking is not stated as a Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: Subnet network address via AND

anchor.snippet: bit masks are used to derive the network address of a subnet

annotations.note: Networking-domain application with no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Curry–Howard: conjunction as product type

anchor.snippet: The Curry–Howard correspondence relates logical conjunction to product types

annotations.note: Lean realizes the correspondence via `And` (in `Prop`) and `Prod` / `PProd` (in `Type`), but no Mathlib theorem states the Curry–Howard equivalence formally.

annotations.label: Intersection via conjunction

anchor.snippet: The membership of an element of an intersection set in set theory is defined in terms of a logical conjunction

annotations.note: `Set.mem_inter_iff : x ∈ s ∩ t ↔ x ∈ s ∧ x ∈ t` defines set-intersection membership via ∧.
